methods.description: Process for collecting continuous terrestrial sediment cores and establishing their chronological framework using radiocarbon dating, archaeological evidence, and stratigraphic correlation.

steps.description: Palaeoenvironmental samples came from two continuous terrestrial cores (N-S3 and C-S1) drilled in 2018. The cores were collected in a SW-NE direction about 150 (N-S3) and 320 (C-S1) meters from the archaeological site. The drilling sites (N-S3: Lat. 44°52′27″ N, Long. 10°34′38″ E; C-S1: Lat. 44°52′32″ N, Long. 10°34′42″ E; Figure 1a) were selected according to geophysical surveys on lithological differences in the sedimentary context of the alluvial plain [45,54].
steps.searchable_summary: Drill continuous terrestrial sediment cores at specified locations based on geophysical surveys.

steps.description: Stratigraphic logs of the N-S3 e C-S1 cores illustrating the different grain size of stratigraphic units are shown in Figure 1b. The deposits consist of alluvial sediments of different grain sizes, from coarse sand to silt and clay, interlayered by buried soil horizons bearing archaeological materials dating to the Bronze Age, and clayey layers enriched in organics formed after decantation in water. From the lithological point of view, deposits mostly consist of siliciclastic sediments with a limited amount of limestone and marl fragments [55].
steps.searchable_summary: Describe and log the stratigraphy and lithology of sediment cores, noting grain size, soil horizons, and materials.

steps.description: The chronology of the sequences... relies on two types of evidence: (i) radiocarbon dates (four dates are published in [54], where the cores are named "S.R. Carotaggio 3" and "S.R. Carotaggio 1"); a new dating for the C-S1 core is published here ( Table 1).
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain radiocarbon dates for core sections.

steps.description: The chronology of the sequences... relies on two types of evidence: ... (ii) ceramic fragments found at 110-130 cm depth into the N-S3 core that have been attributed to the Middle Bronze Age (3200 yr BP) based on typological features.
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and date archaeological materials (ceramic fragments) found in cores based on typological features.

steps.description: Correlations between the accepted dates were made by stratigraphic and sedimentary trends and the pollen assemblage of the sequences [41].
steps.searchable_summary: Correlate chronological data points using stratigraphy, sedimentary trends, and pollen assemblage data.

methods.description: Process for collecting pollen and Rivularia heterocyst samples from sediment cores and performing qualitative and quantitative microscopic analysis, including morphological categorization of heterocysts and comparison with pollen data.

steps.description: Pollen samples were collected throughout the cores (excluding the top levels of arable land) at about 10-30 cm intervals based on the stratigraphy and archaeological phases.
steps.searchable_summary: Collect pollen samples from sediment cores at specified intervals based on stratigraphy and archaeological phases.

steps.description: The N-S3 core was analyzed entirely for pollen and Rivularia type (77 samples) to obtain a complete and long-term reconstruction. According to the detection of vegetation and ecological changes in the most recent phases of the Holocene, heterocysts of the Rivularia type were analyzed qualitatively and quantitatively only in the upper part of the C-S1 (20 samples).
steps.searchable_summary: Collect Rivularia type samples from specified core sections (N-S3 entire, C-S1 upper part).

steps.description: Due to the morphological diversity of the Rivularia type, a systematic quali-quantitative analysis of these structures was carried out on an entire slide for each sample of the N-S3 and the C-S1 cores.
steps.searchable_summary: Perform systematic qualitative and quantitative microscopic analysis of Rivularia heterocysts on entire slides.

steps.description: Heterocysts were divided into categories ( Figure 2; [59]) based on morphology (ellipsoidal or elongated) and the state of preservation of the sheaths surrounding the trichome (presence or absence). The morphological subdivision of the structures is based on the ratio between the major and minor diameters (Md/md) of the heterocyst body. The ellipsoidal shape includes heterocysts of nearly-spherical or spherical shape, while the elongated shape has a greater difference between the sizes of Md and md. Based on the measurements that characterize the elongated and flattened cells, the ratio between the diameters useful for identifying the two morphologies was set at 1.6: minor diameter (md) from ≥1.6 to higher values = elongated heterocysts.
steps.searchable_summary: Categorize Rivularia heterocysts based on morphology (using Md/md ratio with a threshold of 1.6) and sheath preservation.

steps.description: Data on Rivularia heterocysts were also analyzed by comparison with pollen assemblages. The selected pollen sums were considered for the interpretation: (i) hygrophilous trees; (ii) hygrophilous herbs; (iii) hydrophytes.
steps.searchable_summary: Compare Rivularia heterocyst data with selected pollen assemblage data (hygrophilous trees, herbs, hydrophytes).
inputs: selected pollen sums (hygrophilous trees, hygrophilous herbs, hydrophytes)

steps.description: Pollen diagrams were drawn with Tilia [60].
steps.searchable_summary: Generate pollen diagrams using Tilia software.
